title: Anemone
description: I love anemones—their jewel- bright colors are so welcome after a long, dreary winter. Anemones' bold color and shape lend themselves perfectly to a simple, single- flower-type arrangement. They also have a good amount of their own green in the form of a fetching "ruff," which frames their faces. As well as their surprisingly long vase life, anemon
symbolism: Anemones represent unfading love—in Greek mythology, anemones grew from the spilled blood

title: Lily of the Valley
description: Lily-of-the-valley FROM THE FLORIST The best stems to choose will have most of their flowers open, but a few closed buds at the top.
symbolism: Lily-of-the-valley FROM THE FLORIST The best stems to choose will have most of their flowers open, but a few closed

title: Freesia
description: Freesia lactea is a strongly scented species. This variety is called ‘Double White.' Freesias' dainty blooms adorn the top of their elegant, narrow stems in arching clusters. Native to South Africa, freesias have a gorgeous, fruity fragrance. Their upward-facing trumpets come in a range of bright colors, each color producing a slightly different sc

title: Fritillary
description: Fritillaria Snake's head fritillaries' unique, checkerboard pattern will add a touch of class to any arrangement. There are many species of fritillary used for cutting. Some of my favorites are the statuesque and dramatic Persian lily (F. persica) and crown imperial fritillary (F. imperialis), as well as the delicate and wild snake's head fritillar

title: Hellebore
description: Helleborus Hellebores have an understated air that suits the soft light of late winter and early spring. The flowers of the Lenten rose (Helleborus orientalis) are the first cut flowers of the season, and one of the longest lasting in the garden. Even after fertilization, the petals do not fall, but remain to protect the ripening seedhead. Selectiv
symbolism: Display hellebore arrangements on a mantlepiece or high shelf so you can see the flowers'

title: Magnolia
description: Magnolia is one of the grandest ornamental trees, renowned for its striking form and flowers. Magnolia's large, goblet-shaped flowers are various shades of pink, purple, and white, emerging on bare branches in early spring to create a dramatic effect. Their beautiful velvety buds, waxen flowers, and sometimes lichen-encrusted branches are best enjo
symbolism: Hellebore Anemone The twisting stems of the

title: Muscari
description: An easy plant to grow, muscari has perfectly formed, conical- shaped clusters of flowers. Muscari's common name, grape hyacinth, refers to the flowers being similar in appearance to a bunch of grapes. The blooms come in shades of blue, white, and sometimes pink, but few varieties have the stem length necessary for use as a cut flower. The best way

title: Narcissus
description: With its cheery colors and gorgeous scent, narcissus is one of the most popular cut flowers. Narcissus makes up for its limited selection of colors with an amazing variety of forms. These range from the well-known trumpet form of the daffodil, to the unusual split corona—which looks more like an orchid. Lending itself to informal arrangements, narc
symbolism: Narcissus ‘Geranium' is a tazetta-type narcissus, bearing three or four flowers on each stem.

title: Cherry Blossom
description: Cherry Blossom Prunus Brief and beautiful, the eruption of cherry blossom's frothy petals heralds spring like nothing else. The Japanese name for cherry blossom is sakura. In Japan, cherry blossom is revered, symbolizing the transience of life. This is due to the all-too-fleeting nature of its breathtaking beauty—it is not long before the pink and

title: Ranunculus
description: Composed of layer upon layer of tissuelike petals, ranunculus is one of my favorite flowers. Ranunculus comes in an impressive array of shades that vary from vividly bright to soft and muted, so can be used to create a range of moods and aesthetics. As the hollow stems can sometimes struggle to support the heavy heads, place these flowers on the li
symbolism: In the language of flowers, ranunculus symbolizes radiant charm.

title: Pussy Willow
description: Salix At the start of the year, pussy willow is one of the first things I venture into the garden to pick. Pussy willow is known for its soft, silvery-gray catkins that appear on long, dark, slender stems. One of the earliest signs of spring, these catkins provide an important early source of pollen and nectar for bees and other insects. The tactil
symbolism: In Polish folklore, a willow tree pulled a group of drowning kittens out of a river. The furry blooms appear where the

title: Lilac
description: Syringa vulgaris This garden favorite has billowy panicles of richly fragrant flowers. Lilacs bridge the gap between spring and summer. While the flowers are commonly found in a range of lilac and purple shades, pink and white varieties are also available. With their old-fashioned charm, lilacs lend a vintage feel to arrangements, working particula
symbolism: In the language of flowers, lilacs represent newfound love. White lilacs are said to represent innocence.

title: Tulip
description: viridiflora group cultivar that develops a green stripe on its white petals when fully mature. Tulipa A top favorite for many, the tulip celebrates spring with real panache. I always feel spoiled by the overwhelming choice of color and variety this flamboyant flower provides after the limited palette of the winter months. Unlike most other flowers,
symbolism: In 1630s Amsterdam, single tulip bulbs are thought to have been sold for as much as ten times

title: Lady's Mantle
description: Lady's Mantle Lady's Mantle Alchemilla mollis These frothy, green flowers with
symbolism: Lady's Mantle Lady's Mantle Alchemilla mollis

title: Allium
description: With globe-shaped flowers on long, straight stems, alliums create a bold statement. Alliums are an exclamation mark in the garden and in the vase, but take some thought to arrange effectively. Each allium globe is made up of lots of small, star-shaped flowers that, as alliums are part of the onion family, have a slightly oniony scent. As they age,
symbolism: In the language of flowers, alliums symbolize prosperity.

title: Peruvian Lily
description: once known as the "Lily of the Incas." Alstroemeria Flowering for months in the garden and weeks in the vase, Peruvian lilies deserve more recognition. Peruvian lilies are tuberous perennials that originate from South America but, despite their common name, are not related to lilies. At the top of each stem, they produce clusters of flowers that ha
symbolism: Alstroemeria aurea ‘Friendship' is a tall variety for outdoor cultivation, combining soft, buttery yellow with

title: Astrantia
description: Astrantia major Astrantias do not immediately stand out, but a closer look reveals their delicate beauty.
symbolism: Astrantia major Astrantias do not immediately stand out, but a closer look

title: Cornflower
description: Centaurea cyanus Grown en masse, this electric-blue wildflower creates a jaw-dropping effect—the bluest of blues. Also known as "batchelor's buttons," cornflowers have small flower heads that are densely packed with ragged-looking petals on gray-green, wiry stems. Blue is the best-known color, but cornflowers also come in whites, reds, pinks, and p
symbolism: Centaurea cyanus was once widespread, but has now become endangered in its natural environment.

title: Clematis
description: a summer-flowering, herbaceous perennial that produces large, solitary, saucer-shaped flowers. A well-established garden favorite, clematis has now become a florist's staple. With more than 200 varieties, clematis comes in an array of shapes and colors. Different species flower during each season, from the soft pinks of the early-flowering C. monta
symbolism: According to the language of flowers, clematis are associated with art and ingenuity.

title: Delphinium
description: Delphinium's majestic spires never fail to impress with their intense color and bold shape. Their tall stems are densely covered in individual florets that can be found in colors ranging from almost turquoise-blue to the deepest indigo-purples and dusky pinks. Florets can be single or double, the double forms having more petals, and their centers c
symbolism: The individual delphinium florets often have long enough stems to be used in

title: Sweet William
description: Easy to grow from seed, sweet Williams are often better quality home grown or locally purchased. Sweet
symbolism: Easy to grow from seed, sweet Williams are often better quality home grown or locally purchased.

title: Foxglove
description: Digitalis Foxgloves' tall stems are lined with bell-shaped flowers, each with the most exquisite freckling inside. Foxgloves effortlessly naturalize into an arrangement, evoking both woodland glades and cottage gardens due to their association with wildflowers. Cultivated varieties come in a range of pinks, purples, and whites, and are the perfect
symbolism: In the language of flowers, foxgloves represent insincerity.

title: Gypsophila
description: Gypsophila stems bear myriad tiny flowers, creating fluffy clouds of white or pink. Gypsophila has both annual and perennial forms. The annual, G. elegans, smells sweeter, and has shorter, less rigid stems with single flowers that are larger than those of the perennial. The perennial, G. paniculata, bears double flowers, is better known, and is muc
symbolism: Gypsophila elegans ‘Deeprose' is an annual variety, meaning it dies off each year and must be re-sown.

title: Iris
description: for Siberian irises. This variety is called ‘Sparkling Rose.' "Iris" is the Greek word for rainbow.

title: Sweet Pea
description: In the language of flowers, sweet peas are associated with fond farewells. Lathyrus odoratus ‘Winter
symbolism: In the language of flowers, sweet peas are associated with fond farewells.

title: Lavender
description: cleanse, perfume, and annoint since Ancient Egyptian times.

title: Lily
description: Lilium One of the first flowers to be used in floristry, the lily has an exotic air. Of the nine divisions of lilies, Asiatic hybrids and Oriental hybrids are the most commonly used in floristry. Most are grown in large greenhouses under carefully controlled conditions to produce long stems and pristine blooms. They are available in almost every co
symbolism: In Greek mythology, the lily was the symbol of the goddess Hera, and was associated with

title: Nigella
description: develop into seed pods—use this variety in form to add interest to arrangements.
symbolism: Nigella damascena ‘Miss Jekyll Blue' has large, sky-blue, semi-double flowers.

title: Peony
description: Paeonia The most glamorous of cut flowers, peony's lush, abundant blooms always feel like a decadent treat.
symbolism: Paeonia The most glamorous of cut flowers, peony's lush, abundant blooms

title: Icelandic Poppy
description: subarctic climates. However, despite their name, they are not found in Iceland.
symbolism: Icelandic Poppy Papaver nudicaule

title: Rose
description: Rosa The queen of cut flowers, rose's beauty and scent make it many people's favorite flower. This quintessentially English flower comes in a staggering range of varieties. It is available in every color aside from true blue, and its heavenly fragrance is unrivaled. The old-fashioned varieties produce a breathtaking display of highly scented flower
symbolism: Rosa ‘Proper Job' has a quartered- rosette form that is often referred to as “an old-fashioned rose.”

title: Yarrow
description: Achillea Yarrow's color palette reminds me of a Persian rug, with deep reds and pinks to golden yellow.
symbolism: Achillea Yarrow's color palette reminds me of a Persian rug, with deep

title: Love Lies Bleeding
description: associated with eternity due to its long flowering period. Amaranthus caudatus ‘Coral Fountain' is a half-hardy annual with drooping tassels of soft, coral flowers.
symbolism: Love-Lies-Bleeding Love-Lies- Bleeding

title: Snapdragon
description: Antirrhinum majus A garden favorite, snapdragons have statuesque stems lined with velvety flowers.
symbolism: Antirrhinum majus A garden favorite, snapdragons have statuesque

title: Aster
description: Various genera This unpretentious garden stalwart adds color and frothy texture to fall bouquets. The genus Aster has recently been divided into several smaller genera, although many have retained the common name aster. A hardy perennial that bears a froth of small, single or double daisies on multi-branched stems, aster flowers well into the fall
symbolism: In the language of flowers, asters are a symbol of patience.

title: Chrysanthemum
description: Primarily a striking focal flower, chrysanthemum's diversity makes it a versatile flower to arrange. Chrysanthemums come in a huge variety of shapes and sizes, separated into ten categories depending on the form. Florists' chrysanthemums generally have either one large flower head per stem (disbudded stems), or multiple smaller flower heads (spray
symbolism: Chrysanthemum ‘Fuego Bronze' has an intermediate form with two-tone florets.

title: Cosmos
description: Cosmos has an understated elegance with its crinkled petals and open flowers. Two types of cosmos are used as cut flowers.
symbolism: Cosmos has an understated elegance with its crinkled petals and open flowers.

title: Crocosmia
description: Natives to South Africa, crocosmia herald high summer with their blaze of hot colors. Crocosmia, or montbretia, are known for their graceful, arching sprays of brilliantly colored funnel-shaped flowers, accompanied by emerald-green bladelike foliage. The seedheads that appear after flowering are also decorative and dry well. They are one of my favo
symbolism: Crocosmia ‘Lucifer' has furnace-red, trumpetlike flowers on tall, upright stems, and pleated,

title: Dahlia
description: member of the ball group of dahlias. It has good stem length and vase life, so it is an excellent variety for cutting.
symbolism: In the language of flowers, dahlias are a symbol of dignity. No other flower is as generous

title: Sea Holly
description: reach its best color. It can tolerate poor soil, but dislikes waterlogging. Some varieties will self-seed. CONDITIONING Cut stems at an angle and condition them overnight in deep water. DISPLAY Sea holly's spiky thistles contrast well with softer blooms. The larger, bright silver E. giganteum makes an excellent focal flower with its distinctive ruf
symbolism: In the language of flowers, sea holly is a symbol of austerity and independence.

title: Sunflower
description: Helianthus annuus You cannot help but be cheered by the open, sunny face of a sunflower. The best sunflowers for cutting are the annual, pollen-free cultivars. There are many sizes and colors to choose from, including creams, lemons, golds, and reds, as well as bicolored varieties. Plants will often branch willingly if the buds are removed and, if
symbolism: In the language of flowers, tall sunflowers are a symbol of haughtiness, while dwarf sunflowers

title: Hydrangea
description: ‘Grandiflora' is a woody shrub with conical, lace-cap flower heads.
symbolism: Hydrangeas' voluminous flowers change color, aging to beautiful antique shades.

title: Hypericum
description: The glossy, perky hypericum berry is one of my favorite fillers for late summer and fall. Hypericum, or St. John's wort, is covered with a profusion of small, bright-yellow flowers in midsummer. But the plant's main event is the showier berries that follow the flowers in late summer. Clustered on branching woody stems, hypericum berries come in a r
symbolism: Hypericum has long been used as a treatment for mild depression, and as a salve for cuts.

title: Phlox
description: Phlox's big, fluffy, cloud-like heads have a sweet, peppery fragrance. Phlox flower heads are dense panicles made up of large florets. They come in an overwhelming range of varieties and colors. Many cultivars have a darker-colored section in the center of the florets, giving them a two-tone appearance. They are often at their best in mid- to late
symbolism: In the language of flowers, phlox symbolizes harmony.

title: Scabious
description: Scabiosa Scabious's soft color and simple, daisylike form make it the perfect meadow-style flower. The common name, pincushion flower, alludes to the central disk that houses the true flowers. These start as tight buds, giving the impression of lots of tiny pins packed tightly together. The annual variety, S. atropurpurea, has more of a dome-shaped
symbolism: Scabiosa caucasica is a perennial variety with pale blue or lavender- blue flower heads.

title: Zinnia
description: The party animals of the cut-flower world, zinnias appear in a festive array of bright colors. Zinnias are single or double daisylike flowers. Their brightly colored, papery petals and intricate, jewel-like centers make them look almost artificial. There is a fantastic range of varieties available to cut-flower growers, from smaller, long-stemmed v
symbolism: Zinnia elegans ‘Benary's Giant Coral' is a fully double, annual form, meaning it has more petals than single or double forms,

title: Agapanthus
description: Also known as African lily, agapanthus is an exotic beauty in stunning shades of blue or white. Agapanthus flower heads are large, round umbels of trumpet-shaped flowers in shades of light blue to deep purple, and white. These magnificent heads sit upon strong, tall stems above lush, green, straplike foliage. Originally from southern Africa, not al

title: Celosia
description: Celosia's velvety flower head makes it an extraordinary and unique cut flower. This opulent, striking flower comes in a beautiful range of luminous colors. Celosia is categorized into two groups: the Plumosa Group, with its plumelike flowers, and the Cristata or Cockscomb Group, which has crested flowers. Both offer a distinctive textural dimension
symbolism: In Mexico, celosia is known as Flor de terciopelo, meaning “velvet flower.”

title: Jasmine
description: In the language of flowers, white jasmine is a symbol of amiability, and yellow jasmine a symbol of elegance.
symbolism: In the language of flowers, white jasmine is a symbol of amiability, and yellow jasmine

title: Orchid
description: Orchidaceae The diamonds of the cut-flower world, these exotic beauties are often reserved for special events. There are thousands of varieties of orchid. The most commonly available as cut flowers are from the genera Cymbidium, Dendrobium, Oncidium, and Phalaenopsis. These come in every shade and color except true blue. Orchids are usually multico
symbolism: In the language of flowers, orchids are a symbol of refined beauty.
